title: The Rules-and-Facts Model for Simultaneous Generalization and Memorization in Neural Networks
summary: A key capability of modern neural networks is their capacity to simultaneously learn underlying rules and memorize specific facts or exceptions. Yet, theoretical understanding of this dual capability remains limited. We introduce the Rules-and-Facts (RAF) model, a minimal solvable setting that enables precise characterization of this phenomenon by bridging two classical lines of work in the statistical physics of learning: the teacher-student framework for generalization and Gardner-style capacity analysis for memorization. In the RAF model, a fraction $1 - \varepsilon$ of training labels is generated by a structured teacher rule, while a fraction $\varepsilon$ consists of unstructured facts with random labels. We characterize when the learner can simultaneously recover the underlying rule - allowing generalization to new data - and memorize the unstructured examples. Our results quantify how overparameterization enables the simultaneous realization of these two objectives: sufficient excess capacity supports memorization, while regularization and the choice of kernel or nonlinearity control the allocation of capacity between rule learning and memorization. The RAF model provides a theoretical foundation for understanding how modern neural networks can infer structure while storing rare or non-compressible information.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了Rules-and-Facts (RAF) 模型，旨在理解现代神经网络如何同时进行泛化和记忆，并量化了过参数化对实现这两个目标的贡献。
AI.motivation: 现代神经网络能够同时推断结构化模式和存储特定例外或事实，但理论理解仍然有限。本文旨在弥补这一差距，探索泛化和记忆如何在单个架构中协同工作，并解决AI系统产生虚假事实的问题。
AI.method: RAF模型将训练标签分为两部分：一部分由结构化教师规则生成（用于泛化），另一部分是随机标签的事实（用于记忆）。通过分析模型在不同过参数化程度下的表现，研究了模型何时能够同时恢复底层规则和记忆非结构化示例。
AI.result: 研究结果量化了过参数化如何支持同时实现泛化和记忆：足够的过剩容量支持记忆，而正则化和核/非线性函数的选择控制了容量在规则学习和记忆之间的分配。
AI.conclusion: RAF模型为理解现代神经网络如何推断结构并存储稀有或不可压缩信息提供了理论基础，有助于更好地理解泛化和记忆之间的关系，并为构建更可靠的AI系统提供指导。
